sprint: Identity Gate -- Root-Caused Grid Overflow, Sparse Unlit-Star Board, Largest Watchful Lumo, Vocabulary Fix
task: A real user screenshot showed the 10x10 tap grid genuinely clipped (non-square, ~6 of 10 rows visible) plus: 'is it possible to have lumo the guard here. the largest lumo which is watchful. continue is wrong word use vihustudio vocabulary.' Mid-turn: 'in the grid remove the row numbers and make each square unlit star. spread stars sparingly in grid, which are enough to mask the pattern which need to come here.' Root-caused the grid bug via direct measurement (not assumed): every earlier scrollbar sweep this session tested only narrow viewports; a normal WIDE desktop window at moderate height (1280x800 etc) never got tested, and there, flexbox's default flex-shrink silently squished the tap board's HEIGHT (its aspect-ratio:1 flex item, no explicit height) without re-deriving its independently-computed WIDTH, breaking the square and clipping rows via overflow:hidden with zero visible sign. Fixed at the source: flex-shrink:0 on the board wrap (can never be silently squished again) plus a new _fitBoardToAvailableSpace() that MEASURES the panel's real remaining room and, only if genuinely needed, sets the board's width AND height explicitly -- replacing the fragile vh-percentage guessing entirely, called once synchronously on build, again when the Recall path's typed-code fallback expands, and continuously via a self-disconnecting ResizeObserver. Removed the row/column coordinate labels outright. Every cell now carries a static star glyph, invisible at rest by default; a fixed-count (~22%), randomly-positioned sparse subset shows a dim 'unlit' star as pure visual noise (the grid-builder has no idea what the real secret pattern is), so a shoulder-surfer can't tell a genuine tap from background camouflage. Grew Lumo's own clamp() cap from 132px to 180px, safe now that the board correctly reserves room around however big the header is. Renamed 'Continue My Journey' to 'Return to My Adventure', matching the app's own established Adventure vocabulary instead of the generic 'Continue'. Verified via a new 22-assertion suite (zero labels, 100 cells all star-glyphed, a real sparse decoy subset, tap/untap toggling correctly, the board staying genuinely square with zero overflow across 5 realistic desktop sizes and after expanding the typed-code fallback, the vocabulary fix, Lumo's real larger size) plus the full existing 33-assertion suite and every prior narrow-viewport sweep, all passing unchanged, plus a real screenshot. Both goldenBuild.js suites pass unchanged. Confined to css/style.css and js/magicCardUI.js.
